(31) Learning Hexo from Scratch - Extras: Common Questions
This is the post-Ironman wrap-up — but it's actually a list of common Hexo issues you'll run into.
When writing notes, try not to use Hexo's reserved keywords in your headings. A friend in the community ran into this when their heading was
I haven't hit this one myself and I'm not sure how to deliberately reproduce it, but a more common case is double braces (e.g. when you're taking notes about Vue):
If you write that as inline code (single backticks) instead of in a fenced code block (triple backticks), you'll hit an error.
ran clean before, and the only thing that changed was adding a new post that now breaks the build — start looking for these keyword conflicts.

This usually happens when you change the case of a category name and you're using Git for version control. Hexo generates a folder per category for static pages, and Git is not case-sensitive by default — so if your category started as
, the compiled folder might still be
The simplest fix is to nuke the deployed repo and re-upload. The other option is to tweak Git's case sensitivity, which I won't cover here — Google can walk you through it.
file and re-deploy. You can also move the article into
instead if you just want to hide it.
Article content vanishes during dev server
This one's happened to me a few times. If you edit a post while

is running and watching the page, there's a chance the article you're editing suddenly shows up blank. I don't really know why, but when it happens you can hit Ctrl+Z to undo and restore your content.
Best fix: don't write and run the dev server at the same time.
This is the gnarliest process. Basically you have to create a new Hexo project and move your articles over to it (no, really, that's how annoying it is).
The process is fiddly enough that I won't cover it here — I'll write a separate post on my blog later.

This post is a bit on the short side — it's mainly a post-Ironman common-issues supplement, so I didn't plan much content.
I'll write something on upgrading Hexo and NexT next time.
